title: F# Source
documentation:
## Purpose
F# source files contain code written in F#, a functional-first, strongly typed language in the .NET ecosystem developed by Microsoft Research. F# is used for data science, financial modeling, domain-driven design, and any domain that benefits from immutable data and functional composition.

## When to use
Use [F# Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:fsharp;textable;code) for `.fs` implementation files, `.fsi` signature files (which declare the public interface of a module, analogous to C headers), and `.fsx` script files that run without a compilation step.

## Notes
F# source ordering matters: files and declarations within a file must appear before they are used, which is enforced by the compiler. F# interoperates fully with [C# Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:csharp;textable;code) and other .NET languages at the assembly level. The functional style means heavy use of discriminated unions, pattern matching, and pipeline operators, which may require F#-aware parsers for static analysis.
